So when you search something on Wikipedia and you had the Facebook, you don't want to weigh the results as much because you're more interested in the more unique one. So TF-IDF allows us to do this weighting for us.

So we can weigh the vectors using TF-IDF. And the idea is that TF is the term frequency. So how often does this term occur? So if it's quite high? then it's going to be a common word that we use. And then the important part is the inverse document frequency, the IDF part. So if I have a word that is occurring in every document, then it's not important to me because it's not differentiating enough, right? So this occurs in every document.

But if it occurs in very few documents, then, well, this seems like an important word. So it allows me to differentiate different documents because it's rare.
